name: Addison Farmer
abstract: Addison Gerald Farmer (August 21, 1928, Council Bluffs, Iowa – February 20, 1963, New York City) was an American jazz bassist. He was the twin brother of Art Farmer.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: Contemporary Jazz Composers Series
Bel Canto Foundation Jazz Concert to Benefit Musicians

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Golson
about_quotes.evidence: He got his brother, his twin brother, Addison. ... I got Curtis Fuller, and I mentioned McCoy Tyner. ... So I called McCoy. ... Are you interested in joining Art Farmer and me in a group.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Golson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jay McShann
about_quotes.evidence: Pete McShann came back with me then. Pete McShann, Witherspoon, Floating Moody, myself, and Addison Farmer on bass.

about_quotes.speaker: Mose Allison
about_quotes.evidence: I made it with Jerry Segal and Addison Farmer, downtown in a big room...
about_quotes.doc_title: Mose Allison — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Tyner McCoy
about_quotes.evidence: With Benny Golson and Art Farmer and Addison Farmer and what’s his name that played drums
about_quotes.doc_title: Tyner McCoy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
